note: Baseline case — routine monitoring of Russian surface combatants near UK approaches.
Second Royal Navy response in six weeks — signals increased frequency.

assets: Boikiy, Grad, Marshal Ustinov, Severomorsk, others; Merkury, Akademik Nikolaj Strakhov
area: English Channel, North Sea, North Atlantic
response: HMS Tyne, HMS Portland, RAF P-8 Poseidon
note: Multi-zone monitoring — mixes warships with a research vessel.
UK framed as monitoring 'UK waters and adjacent sea areas.'

assets: Kilo-class submarine Novorossiysk; tug Evgeny Churov; corvette Stoiky; tanker MT Yaz
area: Dover Strait, English Channel, North Sea, Atlantic
response: HMS Iron Duke, HMS Tyne, NATO handoffs, RAF
note: Surfaced submarine transit — UK concern extends beyond surface combatants.

assets: Frigate Admiral Golovko; survey ship Yantar; tanker Vyazma
response: HMS Iron Duke, RFA Tideforce, HMS Cattistock
note: Transition from routine shadowing to infrastructure-focused concern.

response: Irish naval and air assets shadowed the vessel
note: Three drones operated near subsea cables and energy infrastructure.

assets: Corvette Soobrazitelny; Sparta II; General Skobelev
area: North Sea → Dover Strait → English Channel
note: Illustrates the cargo and support-shipping dimension of the pattern.

area: UK Exclusive Economic Zone / North Sea (~45 nm off coast)
response: HMS Somerset, HMS Tyne; UK changed rules of engagement
note: Most explicit official UK statement tying Russian activity to critical underwater infrastructure.

assets: Corvette Boikiy; merchant vessel Baltic Leader
note: Escorted logistics and merchant movements included in UK threat picture.

assets: Destroyer Severomorsk; landing ship Alexander Shabalin; Sparta IV; Siyanie Severa
response: HMS Somerset, HMS Cattistock, RFA Tidesurge, helicopter, RAF P-8
note: Repeated Syria-linked return transits — not a one-off.
Reinforces the undersea dimension of the Russian maritime pattern.
Loitered near UK waters following a major NATO exercise.

assets: Survey ship Yantar — used lasers against RAF pilots
area: Edge of British waters, north of Scotland
response: Defence Secretary: UK had 'military options ready'
note: Escalation from surveillance to direct coercive interaction with UK assets.

assets: Corvette Boikiy; oil tanker MT General Skobelev
response: Two Royal Navy patrol ships, Wildcat helicopter

assets: Landing ship Aleksandr Otrakovsky; Sparta IV; Aleksandr Shabalin; MV Sabetta
area: Strait of Gibraltar → Atlantic → Channel → North Sea
response: HMS Cutlass, allied warship, HMS Tyne, Wildcat helicopter
note: Widest geography: full Atlantic-to-Channel monitoring chain.
